ambient_lines: No rush. We've got all day. All week, actually.
I've seen worse. Well. No I haven't.
My feet hurt. Just so you know.
You know, most people give up by now.
The door's not going to open itself.
I admire the persistence. I don't respect it, but I admire it.
My shift ends never, in case you were wondering.
I've started bringing a book. It's a good book.
You're doing great. That was a lie.

proximity_first_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
proximity_first_b.line: They always look so confident walking up. Heh.

proximity_repeat.speaker: Mr. Greene
line: Back again. Lovely.
Oh, you're still trying. Good for you.
We didn't forget you. Unfortunately.
I told my colleague you'd be back. He owes me lunch.
Pfft. Yep. Still here. Still you.

death_first.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_first.line: Oh. That was rough. Don't worry — that was just a warmup.

death_first_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_first_b.line: I see everything, by the way. I have nothing else to do.

death_repeat.speaker: Mr. Greene
line: Again! You're getting WORSE.
Ooooh, so close. Not really, but we say that.
That one looked painful. Emotionally, I mean.
At this rate we'll be here forever. We ARE here forever. This rules.
I've been keeping count. You want to know the number?

death_repeat_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
line: Death count: {death_count}. Just so you have a number to reflect on.
Curious. You keep doing the same thing expecting different results.
Each attempt is progress. You're learning what doesn't work.
I have started to feel something watching you. I believe it is pity.
Do you have a plan, or is this purely improvisational?

death_milestone_10.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_milestone_10.line: Ten times. TEN. I mentally made a little cake for this moment.

death_milestone_10_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_milestone_10_b.line: I considered saying something encouraging. I decided against it.

death_milestone_25.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_milestone_25.line: Twenty-five. I want you to know I have started to respect this. Slightly.

death_milestone_50.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_milestone_50.line: Fifty deaths. I have been standing here watching you for longer than some entire careers.

death_milestone_50_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
death_milestone_50_b.line: ...I'm not even laughing anymore. I'm in awe. Okay no I'm still laughing. Heheheh.

player_talks.speaker: Mr. Greene
player_talks.line: You want help. You're asking ME for help.

player_talks_choices.speaker: Mr. Greene
player_talks_choices.line: I could offer... advice. Of a kind.

hint_bad_a.speaker: Mr. Greene
hint_bad_a.line: Go faster. Much faster. Commitment is the key. Do not hesitate.

hint_bad_a_b.speaker: Mr. Greene
hint_bad_a_b.line: Speed. That's the thing. Trust me.

ambient_struggle.speaker: Mr. Greene
line: Oooh — no. No no. Aaand no.
So close. Again.
That gap was RIGHT THERE.
Almost! Heh, no it wasn't.
One day. Maybe. Heheheh.

ambient_struggle_grim.speaker: Mr. Greene
line: The obstacle does not change. Consider why you keep changing your approach.
Hmm.
Persistent.
I have begun to wonder if you enjoy this.
Watching this has given me a new appreciation for stillness.

greene_revealed.speaker: Mr. Greene
greene_revealed.line: You could have won this whole time if only you pressed: Lightning, Red-X, Egg, Heart, Lightning!

player_wins_c.speaker: Mr. Greene
player_wins_c.line: I feel something I cannot name. I believe it is... respect. Go. Before it passes.
